{{Infobox religious building|image=Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (4).jpg |名称 = Yurahime Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |所在地 = [[Shimane Prefecture]], [[Oki District]], [[Nishinoshima Town]], Urago 922 |位置 = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[Suseri-hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]] |社格 = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinai-sha]] ([[Myojin Taisha|Myojin Taisha]])
[[Ichinomiya]] of [[Oki Province]]
Former [[Gosha]] |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Modified Kasuga-zukuri |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Last Saturday and Sunday of July |神事 = Kami-gaeri Festival (November 29) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}}}} '''Yurahime Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a Shinto shrine]] in Oki District, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a [[Myojin Taisha]]. It claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[Oki Province]], but [[Mizuwakasu Shrine]] also claims this. [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:由良比女神社]] [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社, Yurahime Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Urago, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinai-sha]] (a shrine listed in the Engishiki), specifically a [[Myojin Taisha|Myojin Taisha]], and it is also the [[Ichinomiya]] (highest ranking shrine) of [[Oki Province]]. Its former rank under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern shrine ranking system]] was that of a [[Gosha]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deity: * [[Suseri-hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]], locally known as Yurahime no Okami. According to the [[Engishiki]], the shrine was originally known as "Watasu Shrine," and the enshrined deity is believed to be a sea deity. It is also referred to as "Chiburi no Kami" in historical texts such as the [[Soden Chō]] and the [[Tosa Nikki]]. Local tradition on the island of Dozen tells that long ago, while Suserihime no Mikoto was crossing the sea in a tub, a squid pulled her hand (or bit her hand), and as an apology, squids began to gather annually at the beach in front of the shrine. == History == === Overview === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the [[Shoku Nihongi]], in the ninth year of [[Shōwa (Ninth century Japan)|Shōwa]] (842 AD), Yurahime Shrine, along with [[Ujukami Shrine]] on [[Nakanoshima (Shimane)|Nakanoshima]] and [[Mizuwakasu Shrine]] on [[Dōgo (island)|Dōgo]], was designated as a government-supported shrine. The [[Engishiki]] Jinmyocho, compiled in the fifth year of [[Enchō (Era)|Enchō]] (927 AD), lists Yurahime Shrine as a Myojin Taisha in [[Chibu District]], Oki Province. The [[Oki Kokunai Jinja Chō]] refers to it as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyojin." The shrine experienced a decline from the medieval period to the mid-Edo period. In the second year of [[An'ei]] (1773), the festival was revived by the village heads of the 13 villages of Dozen. Since the early modern period, the shrine has claimed the status of Ichinomiya of Oki Province. In the fifth year of [[Meiji]] (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Gosha. The shrine grounds and buildings were expanded and developed after 1889. === Shrine Ranking === * Jusanmi no Kami (従三位上) - As listed in the Oki Kokunai Jinja Chō. == Festivals == === Rituals === * Yurahime Shrine Grand Festival Formerly held biennially on July 28 and 29, it is now celebrated on the last Saturday and Sunday nights of July in odd-numbered years. The portable shrine is placed on a godship formed by connecting two large fishing boats. Island dances are performed on the ship as it departs from Yura and crosses the sea to Urago Bay. After parading through the town, the portable shrine stays overnight at the shrine priest's residence and returns to the shrine the following night. This procession is one of the grandest festivals on the Oki Islands, featuring vibrant participation by young men carrying the portable shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (designated as a cultural property by Nishinoshima Town)}}]] * Main Hall *: Built in the 22nd year of Meiji (1889). It features a modified [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style with a Karahafu-style gabled roof. Previously described as "Taisha-zukuri variant," it is now classified as "Kasuga-zukuri variant." Locally, in the [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] region, this style is called "Myōjin-zukuri." It is designated as a tangible cultural property by Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Shrine buildings
{{small|West side of the worship hall. From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Miya, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii Gate
{{small|Located beside Prefectural Road 315. The Zuijin Gate and the second Torii gate are visible in the background.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuijin Gate
{{small|Left and right statues of ministers on either side of the approach.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo ring
 == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Nishinoshima Town === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Main Hall (Building) - Designated on March 7, 1982[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html Nishinoshima Town Designated Cultural Properties List] Shimane Prefecture. == Legend == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach (Squid Gathering Beach)}}]] The beach in front of the shrine, with its sea torii gate, is called "Squid Gathering Beach," as squids gather here from October to February. According to the legend from Chibu Village, after Yurahime Shrine was relocated from Ika Beach in Chibu Village to Yura, squids began to gather at Yura. It is said that when the deity Yurahime was traveling across the sea in a tub, a squid pulled or bit her hand while she was washing it in the sea. As an apology, a large number of squids began to gather at the beach in front of the shrine every autumn. The locals considered this a miraculous sign and built small huts along the water's edge to collect the squids with their families. Originally, Yurahime Shrine was located at Furumi in Chiburijima and was moved to Urago, after which the squids stopped gathering in Chiburijima. Another story claims that people from Nishinoshima envied the squids gathering in Chiburijima and stole the deity, relocating it to Urago. The deity is said to have appeared near the shrine's torii gate on a tatami stone, holding a squid in her hand while riding on a tub, during the reign of Emperor Jimmu. According to another version, the deity is Suserihime no Mikoto. She got angry when squids pulled her hand while she was crossing the sea to Urago. The squid gathering is considered a sign of "Kami-gaeri" (God's return), which is celebrated on October 29th with a ritual. The deity is also called "Surume Daimyōjin" due to this connection with squids. == Related Items == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki Dozen Kagura performed at Yurahime Shrine]] * [[Oki Dozen Kagura]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{Jinja-Shiryō-Shūsei|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}} [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Gosha Shrines]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]]

{{Infobox religious building|image=Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (4).jpg |名称 = Yurahime Shrine |画像 = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |所在地 = [[Shimane Prefecture]], [[Oki District]], [[Nishinoshima Town]], Urago 922 |位置 = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[Suseri-hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]] |社格 = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinai-sha]] ([[Myojin Taisha|Myojin Taisha]])
[[Ichinomiya]] of [[Oki Province]]
Former [[Gosha]] |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Modified Kasuga-zukuri |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Last Saturday and Sunday of July |神事 = Kami-gaeri festival (November 29) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}}}} '''Yurahime Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a Shinto shrine]] in Oki District, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a [[Myojin Taisha]]. It claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[Oki Province]], but [[Mizuwakasu Shrine]] also claims this. [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:由良比女神社]] [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社, Yurahime Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Urago, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinai-sha]] (a shrine listed in the Engishiki), specifically a [[Myojin Taisha|Myojin Taisha]], and it is also the [[Ichinomiya]] (highest ranking shrine) of [[Oki Province]]. Its former rank under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern shrine ranking system]] was that of a [[Gosha]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deity: * [[Suseri-hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]], locally known as Yurahime no Okami. According to the [[Engishiki]], the shrine was originally known as "Watasu Shrine," and the enshrined deity is believed to be a sea deity. It is also referred to as "Chiburi no Kami" in historical texts such as the [[Soden Chō]] and the [[Tosa Nikki]]. Local tradition on the island of Dozen tells that long ago, while Suserihime no Mikoto was crossing the sea in a tub, a squid pulled her hand (or bit her hand), and as an apology, squids began to gather annually at the beach in front of the shrine. == History == === Overview === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the [[Shoku Nihongi]], in the ninth year of [[Shōwa (Ninth century Japan)|Shōwa]] (842 AD), Yurahime Shrine, along with [[Ujukami Shrine]] on [[Nakanoshima (Shimane)|Nakanoshima]] and [[Mizuwakasu Shrine]] on [[Dōgo (island)|Dōgo]], was designated as a government-supported shrine. The [[Engishiki]] Jinmyocho, compiled in the fifth year of [[Enchō (Era)|Enchō]] (927 AD), lists Yurahime Shrine as a Myojin Taisha in [[Chibu District]], Oki Province. The [[Oki Kokunai Jinja Chō]] refers to it as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyojin." The shrine experienced a decline from the medieval period to the mid-Edo period. In the second year of [[An'ei]] (1773), the festival was revived by the village heads of the 13 villages of Dozen. Since the early modern period, the shrine has claimed the status of Ichinomiya of Oki Province. In the fifth year of [[Meiji]] (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Gosha. The shrine grounds and buildings were expanded and developed after 1889. === Shrine ranking === * Jusanmi no Kami (従三位上) - As listed in the Oki Kokunai Jinja Chō. == festivals == === Rituals === * Yurahime Shrine Grand festival Formerly held biennially on July 28 and 29, it is now celebrated on the last Saturday and Sunday nights of July in odd-numbered years. The portable shrine is placed on a godship formed by connecting two large fishing boats. Island dances are performed on the ship as it departs from Yura and crosses the sea to Urago Bay. After parading through the town, the portable shrine stays overnight at the shrine priest's residence and returns to the shrine the following night. This procession is one of the grandest festivals on the Oki Islands, featuring vibrant participation by young men carrying the portable shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|main hall (designated as a cultural property by Nishinoshima Town)}}]] * main hall *: Built in the 22nd year of Meiji (1889). It features a modified [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style with a Karahafu-style gabled roof. Previously described as "Taisha-zukuri variant," it is now classified as "Kasuga-zukuri variant." Locally, in the [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] region, this style is called "Myōjin-zukuri." It is designated as a tangible cultural property by Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Shrine buildings
{{small|West side of the worship hall. From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Miya, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii Gate
{{small|Located beside Prefectural Road 315. The Zuijin Gate and the second Torii gate are visible in the background.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuijin Gate
{{small|Left and right statues of ministers on either side of the approach.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo ring
 == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Nishinoshima Town === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** main hall (Building) - Designated on March 7, 1982[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html Nishinoshima Town Designated Cultural Properties List] Shimane Prefecture. == Legend == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach (Squid Gathering Beach)}}]] The beach in front of the shrine, with its sea torii gate, is called "Squid Gathering Beach," as squids gather here from October to February. According to the legend from Chibu Village, after Yurahime Shrine was relocated from Ika Beach in Chibu Village to Yura, squids began to gather at Yura. It is said that when the deity Yurahime was traveling across the sea in a tub, a squid pulled or bit her hand while she was washing it in the sea. As an apology, a large number of squids began to gather at the beach in front of the shrine every autumn. The locals considered this a miraculous sign and built small huts along the water's edge to collect the squids with their families. Originally, Yurahime Shrine was located at Furumi in Chiburijima and was moved to Urago, after which the squids stopped gathering in Chiburijima. Another story claims that people from Nishinoshima envied the squids gathering in Chiburijima and stole the deity, relocating it to Urago. The deity is said to have appeared near the shrine's torii gate on a tatami stone, holding a squid in her hand while riding on a tub, during the reign of Emperor Jimmu. According to another version, the deity is Suserihime no Mikoto. She got angry when squids pulled her hand while she was crossing the sea to Urago. The squid gathering is considered a sign of "Kami-gaeri" (God's return), which is celebrated on October 29th with a ritual. The deity is also called "Surume Daimyōjin" due to this connection with squids. == Related Items == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki Dozen Kagura performed at Yurahime Shrine]] * [[Oki Dozen Kagura]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{Jinja-Shiryō-Shūsei|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}} [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Gosha Shrines]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]]

Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}}}} '''Yurahime Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a Shinto shrine]] in Oki District, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a [[Myojin Taisha]]. It claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[Oki Province]], but [[Mizuwakasu Shrine]] also claims this. [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:由良比女神社]] [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社, Yurahime Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Urago, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinai-sha]] (a shrine listed in the Engishiki), specifically a [[Myojin Taisha|Myojin Taisha]], and it is also the [[Ichinomiya]] (highest ranking shrine) of [[Oki Province]]. Its former rank under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern shrine ranking system]] was that of a [[Gosha]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deity: * [[Suseri-hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]], locally known as Yurahime no Okami. According to the [[Engishiki]], the shrine was originally known as "Watasu Shrine," and the enshrined deity is believed to be a sea deity. It is also referred to as "Chiburi no Kami" in historical texts such as the [[Soden Chō]] and the [[Tosa Nikki]]. Local tradition on the island of Dozen tells that long ago, while Suserihime no Mikoto was crossing the sea in a tub, a squid pulled her hand (or bit her hand), and as an apology, squids began to gather annually at the beach in front of the shrine. == History == === Overview === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the [[Shoku Nihongi]], in the ninth year of [[Shōwa (Ninth century Japan)|Shōwa]] (842 AD), Yurahime Shrine, along with [[Ujukami Shrine]] on [[Nakanoshima (Shimane)|Nakanoshima]] and [[Mizuwakasu Shrine]] on [[Dōgo (island)|Dōgo]], was designated as a government-supported shrine. The [[Engishiki]] Jinmyocho, compiled in the fifth year of [[Enchō (Era)|Enchō]] (927 AD), lists Yurahime Shrine as a Myojin Taisha in [[Chibu District]], Oki Province. The [[Oki Kokunai Jinja Chō]] refers to it as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyojin." The shrine experienced a decline from the medieval period to the mid-Edo period. In the second year of [[An'ei]] (1773), the festival was revived by the village heads of the 13 villages of Dozen. Since the early modern period, the shrine has claimed the status of Ichinomiya of Oki Province. In the fifth year of [[Meiji]] (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Gosha. The shrine grounds and buildings were expanded and developed after 1889. === Shrine ranking === * Jusanmi no Kami (従三位上) - As listed in the Oki Kokunai Jinja Chō. == festivals == === Rituals === * Yurahime Shrine Grand festival Formerly held biennially on July 28 and 29, it is now celebrated on the last Saturday and Sunday nights of July in odd-numbered years. The portable shrine is placed on a godship formed by connecting two large fishing boats. Island dances are performed on the ship as it departs from Yura and crosses the sea to Urago Bay. After parading through the town, the portable shrine stays overnight at the shrine priest's residence and returns to the shrine the following night. This procession is one of the grandest festivals on the Oki Islands, featuring vibrant participation by young men carrying the portable shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|main hall (designated as a cultural property by Nishinoshima Town)}}]] * main hall *: Built in the 22nd year of Meiji (1889). It features a modified [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style with a Karahafu-style gabled roof. Previously described as "Taisha-zukuri variant," it is now classified as "Kasuga-zukuri variant." Locally, in the [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] region, this style is called "Myōjin-zukuri." It is designated as a tangible cultural property by Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Shrine buildings
{{small|West side of the worship hall. From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Miya, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii Gate
{{small|Located beside Prefectural Road 315. The Zuijin Gate and the second Torii gate are visible in the background.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuijin Gate
{{small|Left and right statues of ministers on either side of the approach.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo ring
 == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Nishinoshima Town === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** main hall (Building) - Designated on March 7, 1982[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html Nishinoshima Town Designated Cultural Properties List] Shimane Prefecture. == Legend == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach (Squid Gathering Beach)}}]] The beach in front of the shrine, with its sea torii gate, is called "Squid Gathering Beach," as squids gather here from October to February. According to the legend from Chibu Village, after Yurahime Shrine was relocated from Ika Beach in Chibu Village to Yura, squids began to gather at Yura. It is said that when the deity Yurahime was traveling across the sea in a tub, a squid pulled or bit her hand while she was washing it in the sea. As an apology, a large number of squids began to gather at the beach in front of the shrine every autumn. The locals considered this a miraculous sign and built small huts along the water's edge to collect the squids with their families. Originally, Yurahime Shrine was located at Furumi in Chiburijima and was moved to Urago, after which the squids stopped gathering in Chiburijima. Another story claims that people from Nishinoshima envied the squids gathering in Chiburijima and stole the deity, relocating it to Urago. The deity is said to have appeared near the shrine's torii gate on a tatami stone, holding a squid in her hand while riding on a tub, during the reign of Emperor Jimmu. According to another version, the deity is Suserihime no Mikoto. She got angry when squids pulled her hand while she was crossing the sea to Urago. The squid gathering is considered a sign of "Kami-gaeri" (God's return), which is celebrated on October 29th with a ritual. The deity is also called "Surume Daimyōjin" due to this connection with squids. == Related Items == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki Dozen Kagura performed at Yurahime Shrine]] * [[Oki Dozen Kagura]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{Jinja-Shiryō-Shūsei|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}} [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Gosha Shrines]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]]

Local tradition on the island of Dozen tells that long ago, while Suserihime no Mikoto was crossing the sea in a tub, a squid pulled her hand (or bit her hand), and as an apology, squids began to gather annually at the beach in front of the shrine. == History == === Overview === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the [[Shoku Nihongi]], in the ninth year of [[Shōwa (Ninth century Japan)|Shōwa]] (842 AD), Yurahime Shrine, along with [[Ujukami Shrine]] on [[Nakanoshima (Shimane)|Nakanoshima]] and [[Mizuwakasu Shrine]] on [[Dōgo (island)|Dōgo]], was designated as a government-supported shrine. The [[Engishiki]] Jinmyocho, compiled in the fifth year of [[Enchō (Era)|Enchō]] (927 AD), lists Yurahime Shrine as a Myojin Taisha in [[Chibu District]], Oki Province. The [[Oki Kokunai Jinja Chō]] refers to it as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyojin." The shrine experienced a decline from the medieval period to the mid-Edo period. In the second year of [[An'ei]] (1773), the festival was revived by the village heads of the 13 villages of Dozen. Since the early modern period, the shrine has claimed the status of Ichinomiya of Oki Province. In the fifth year of [[Meiji]] (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Gosha. The shrine grounds and buildings were expanded and developed after 1889. === Shrine ranking === * Jusanmi no Kami (従三位上) - As listed in the Oki Kokunai Jinja Chō. == festivals == === Rituals === * Yurahime Shrine Grand festival Formerly held biennially on July 28 and 29, it is now celebrated on the last Saturday and Sunday nights of July in odd-numbered years. The portable shrine is placed on a godship formed by connecting two large fishing boats. Island dances are performed on the ship as it departs from Yura and crosses the sea to Urago Bay. After parading through the town, the portable shrine stays overnight at the shrine priest's residence and returns to the shrine the following night. This procession is one of the grandest festivals on the Oki Islands, featuring vibrant participation by young men carrying the portable shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|main hall (designated as a cultural property by Nishinoshima Town)}}]] * main hall *: Built in the 22nd year of Meiji (1889). It features a modified [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style with a Karahafu-style gabled roof. Previously described as "Taisha-zukuri variant," it is now classified as "Kasuga-zukuri variant." Locally, in the [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] region, this style is called "Myōjin-zukuri." It is designated as a tangible cultural property by Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Shrine buildings
{{small|West side of the worship hall. From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Miya, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii Gate
{{small|Located beside Prefectural Road 315. The Zuijin Gate and the second Torii gate are visible in the background.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuijin Gate
{{small|Left and right statues of ministers on either side of the approach.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo ring
 == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Nishinoshima Town === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** main hall (Building) - Designated on March 7, 1982[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html Nishinoshima Town Designated Cultural Properties List] Shimane Prefecture. == Legend == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach (Squid Gathering Beach)}}]] The beach in front of the shrine, with its sea torii gate, is called "Squid Gathering Beach," as squids gather here from October to February. According to the legend from Chibu Village, after Yurahime Shrine was relocated from Ika Beach in Chibu Village to Yura, squids began to gather at Yura. It is said that when the deity Yurahime was traveling across the sea in a tub, a squid pulled or bit her hand while she was washing it in the sea. As an apology, a large number of squids began to gather at the beach in front of the shrine every autumn. The locals considered this a miraculous sign and built small huts along the water's edge to collect the squids with their families. Originally, Yurahime Shrine was located at Furumi in Chiburijima and was moved to Urago, after which the squids stopped gathering in Chiburijima. Another story claims that people from Nishinoshima envied the squids gathering in Chiburijima and stole the deity, relocating it to Urago. The deity is said to have appeared near the shrine's torii gate on a tatami stone, holding a squid in her hand while riding on a tub, during the reign of Emperor Jimmu. According to another version, the deity is Suserihime no Mikoto. She got angry when squids pulled her hand while she was crossing the sea to Urago. The squid gathering is considered a sign of "Kami-gaeri" (God's return), which is celebrated on October 29th with a ritual. The deity is also called "Surume Daimyōjin" due to this connection with squids. == Related Items == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki Dozen Kagura performed at Yurahime Shrine]] * [[Oki Dozen Kagura]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{Jinja-Shiryō-Shūsei|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}} [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Gosha Shrines]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]]

After parading through the town, the portable shrine stays overnight at the shrine priest's residence and returns to the shrine the following night. This procession is one of the grandest festivals on the Oki Islands, featuring vibrant participation by young men carrying the portable shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|main hall (designated as a cultural property by Nishinoshima Town)}}]] * main hall *: Built in the 22nd year of Meiji (1889). It features a modified [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style with a Karahafu-style gabled roof. Previously described as "Taisha-zukuri variant," it is now classified as "Kasuga-zukuri variant." Locally, in the [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] region, this style is called "Myōjin-zukuri." It is designated as a tangible cultural property by Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Shrine buildings
{{small|West side of the worship hall. From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Miya, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii Gate
{{small|Located beside Prefectural Road 315. The Zuijin Gate and the second Torii gate are visible in the background.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuijin Gate
{{small|Left and right statues of ministers on either side of the approach.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo ring
 == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Nishinoshima Town === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** main hall (Building) - Designated on March 7, 1982[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html Nishinoshima Town Designated Cultural Properties List] Shimane Prefecture. == Legend == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach (Squid Gathering Beach)}}]] The beach in front of the shrine, with its sea torii gate, is called "Squid Gathering Beach," as squids gather here from October to February. According to the legend from Chibu Village, after Yurahime Shrine was relocated from Ika Beach in Chibu Village to Yura, squids began to gather at Yura. It is said that when the deity Yurahime was traveling across the sea in a tub, a squid pulled or bit her hand while she was washing it in the sea. As an apology, a large number of squids began to gather at the beach in front of the shrine every autumn. The locals considered this a miraculous sign and built small huts along the water's edge to collect the squids with their families. Originally, Yurahime Shrine was located at Furumi in Chiburijima and was moved to Urago, after which the squids stopped gathering in Chiburijima. Another story claims that people from Nishinoshima envied the squids gathering in Chiburijima and stole the deity, relocating it to Urago. The deity is said to have appeared near the shrine's torii gate on a tatami stone, holding a squid in her hand while riding on a tub, during the reign of Emperor Jimmu. According to another version, the deity is Suserihime no Mikoto. She got angry when squids pulled her hand while she was crossing the sea to Urago. The squid gathering is considered a sign of "Kami-gaeri" (God's return), which is celebrated on October 29th with a ritual. The deity is also called "Surume Daimyōjin" due to this connection with squids. == Related Items == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki Dozen Kagura performed at Yurahime Shrine]] * [[Oki Dozen Kagura]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{Jinja-Shiryō-Shūsei|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}} [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Gosha Shrines]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]]

Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]], [[Oki District]], [[Nishinoshima Town]], Urago 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Suseri-hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]] |rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinai-sha]] ([[Myojin Taisha|Myojin Taisha]])
[[Ichinomiya]] of [[Oki Province]]
Former [[Gosha]] |established = Unknown |main hall = Modified Kasuga-zukuri |alternative name = |札所等 = |festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |festival = Kami-gaeri festival (November 29) |map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}}}} '''Yurahime Shrine''' is [[Shinto shrine|a Shinto shrine]] in Oki District, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a [[Myojin Taisha]]. It claims to be the [[Ichinomiya]] of [[Oki Province]], but [[Mizuwakasu Shrine]] also claims this. [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:由良比女神社]] [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社, Yurahime Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Urago, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is classified as a [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinai-sha]] (a shrine listed in the Engishiki), specifically a [[Myojin Taisha|Myojin Taisha]], and it is also the [[Ichinomiya]] (highest ranking shrine) of [[Oki Province]]. Its former rank under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern shrine ranking system]] was that of a [[Gosha]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deity: * [[Suseri-hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]], locally known as Yurahime no Okami. According to the [[Engishiki]], the shrine was originally known as "Watasu Shrine," and the enshrined deity is believed to be a sea deity. It is also referred to as "Chiburi no Kami" in historical texts such as the [[Soden Chō]] and the [[Tosa Nikki]]. Local tradition on the island of Dozen tells that long ago, while Suserihime no Mikoto was crossing the sea in a tub, a squid pulled her hand (or bit her hand), and as an apology, squids began to gather annually at the beach in front of the shrine. == History == === Overview === The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to the [[Shoku Nihongi]], in the ninth year of [[Shōwa (Ninth century Japan)|Shōwa]] (842 AD), Yurahime Shrine, along with [[Ujukami Shrine]] on [[Nakanoshima (Shimane)|Nakanoshima]] and [[Mizuwakasu Shrine]] on [[Dōgo (island)|Dōgo]], was designated as a government-supported shrine. The [[Engishiki]] Jinmyocho, compiled in the fifth year of [[Enchō (Era)|Enchō]] (927 AD), lists Yurahime Shrine as a Myojin Taisha in [[Chibu District]], Oki Province. The [[Oki Kokunai Jinja Chō]] refers to it as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyojin." The shrine experienced a decline from the medieval period to the mid-Edo period. In the second year of [[An'ei]] (1773), the festival was revived by the village heads of the 13 villages of Dozen. Since the early modern period, the shrine has claimed the status of Ichinomiya of Oki Province. In the fifth year of [[Meiji]] (1872), the shrine was ranked as a Gosha. The shrine grounds and buildings were expanded and developed after 1889. === Shrine ranking === * Jusanmi no Kami (従三位上) - As listed in the Oki Kokunai Jinja Chō. == festivals == === Rituals === * Yurahime Shrine Grand festival Formerly held biennially on July 28 and 29, it is now celebrated on the last Saturday and Sunday nights of July in odd-numbered years. The portable shrine is placed on a godship formed by connecting two large fishing boats. Island dances are performed on the ship as it departs from Yura and crosses the sea to Urago Bay. After parading through the town, the portable shrine stays overnight at the shrine priest's residence and returns to the shrine the following night. This procession is one of the grandest festivals on the Oki Islands, featuring vibrant participation by young men carrying the portable shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|main hall (designated as a cultural property by Nishinoshima Town)}}]] * main hall *: Built in the 22nd year of Meiji (1889). It features a modified [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style with a Karahafu-style gabled roof. Previously described as "Taisha-zukuri variant," it is now classified as "Kasuga-zukuri variant." Locally, in the [[Izumo Province|Izumo]] region, this style is called "Myōjin-zukuri." It is designated as a tangible cultural property by Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Shrine buildings
{{small|West side of the worship hall. From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Miya, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii Gate
{{small|Located beside Prefectural Road 315. The Zuijin Gate and the second Torii gate are visible in the background.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuijin Gate
{{small|Left and right statues of ministers on either side of the approach.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo ring
 == Cultural Properties == === Designated Cultural Properties of Nishinoshima Town === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** main hall (Building) - Designated on March 7, 1982[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html Nishinoshima Town Designated Cultural Properties List] Shimane Prefecture. == Legend == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach (Squid Gathering Beach)}}]] The beach in front of the shrine, with its sea torii gate, is called "Squid Gathering Beach," as squids gather here from October to February. According to the legend from Chibu Village, after Yurahime Shrine was relocated from Ika Beach in Chibu Village to Yura, squids began to gather at Yura. It is said that when the deity Yurahime was traveling across the sea in a tub, a squid pulled or bit her hand while she was washing it in the sea. As an apology, a large number of squids began to gather at the beach in front of the shrine every autumn. The locals considered this a miraculous sign and built small huts along the water's edge to collect the squids with their families. Originally, Yurahime Shrine was located at Furumi in Chiburijima and was moved to Urago, after which the squids stopped gathering in Chiburijima. Another story claims that people from Nishinoshima envied the squids gathering in Chiburijima and stole the deity, relocating it to Urago. The deity is said to have appeared near the shrine's torii gate on a tatami stone, holding a squid in her hand while riding on a tub, during the reign of Emperor Jimmu. According to another version, the deity is Suserihime no Mikoto. She got angry when squids pulled her hand while she was crossing the sea to Urago. The squid gathering is considered a sign of "Kami-gaeri" (God's return), which is celebrated on October 29th with a ritual. The deity is also called "Surume Daimyōjin" due to this connection with squids. == Related Items == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki Dozen Kagura performed at Yurahime Shrine]] * [[Oki Dozen Kagura]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{Jinja-Shiryō-Shūsei|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}} [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Gosha Shrines]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]]

{{Shrine |Name = Yurahime Shrine |Image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |Location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |Coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
{{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |Annual Festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭}} (November 29) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine Annual Festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine Annual Festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. {{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == Related Topics == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Gosha Shrines]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]

Worship Hall |Location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |Coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |Annual Festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭}} (November 29) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine Annual Festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines

The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine Annual Festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == Related Topics == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]

Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine Annual Festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine Annual Festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == Related Topics == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]

Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |Name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |Coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |Annual Festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine Annual Festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == Related Topics == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]
