{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (4).jpg |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]], [[Oki District]], [[Nishinoshima Town]], Urago 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Suseri-hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]] |rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinai-sha]] ([[Myojin Taisha|Myojin Taisha]])
[[Ichinomiya]] of [[Oki Province]]
Former [[Gosha]] |established = Unknown |main hall = Modified Kasuga-zukuri |alternative name = |札所等 = |festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |festival = Kami-gaeri festival (November 29) |map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
{{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|隠岐国}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|隠岐国内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}})
{{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii

{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines

Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216== See Also == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (4).jpg |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]], [[Oki District]], [[Nishinoshima Town]], Urago 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Suseri-hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]] |rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinai-sha]] ([[Myojin Taisha|Myojin Taisha]])
[[Ichinomiya]] of [[Oki Province]]
Former [[Gosha]] |established = Unknown |main hall = Modified Kasuga-zukuri |alternative name = |札所等 = |festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |festival = Kami-gaeri festival (November 29) |map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})
{{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also! == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[Ichinomiya]] of [[Oki Province]]
Former [[Gosha]] |established = Unknown |main hall = Modified Kasuga-zukuri |alternative name = |札所等 = |festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |festival = Kami-gaeri festival (November 29) |map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}})

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province }}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭|12=draft}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|12=draft}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=draft}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|12=draft}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|12=draft}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=draft}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|12=draft}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|12=draft}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|12=draft}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|12=draft}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|12=draft}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=draft}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|12=draft}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|12=draft}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|12=draft}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|12=draft}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|12=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}}

In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|12=draft}}. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|12=draft}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|12=draft}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|12=draft}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|12=draft}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|12=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}}
