Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭|12=draft}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|12=draft}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=draft}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|12=draft}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|12=draft}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=draft}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|12=draft}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|12=draft}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|12=draft}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|12=draft}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|12=draft}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=draft}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|12=draft}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|12=draft}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|12=draft}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|12=draft}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|12=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}}

Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}})
{{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭|12=draft}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|12=draft}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=draft}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|12=draft}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|12=draft}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=draft}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|12=draft}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|12=draft}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|12=draft}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|12=draft}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|12=draft}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=draft}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|12=draft}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|12=draft}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|12=draft}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|12=draft}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|12=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}}

In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|12=draft}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|12=draft}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|12=draft}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|12=draft}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|12=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (4).jpg |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]], [[Oki District]], [[Nishinoshima Town]], Urago 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Suseri-hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]] |rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinai-sha]] ([[Myojin Taisha|Myojin Taisha]])
[[Ichinomiya]] of [[Oki Province]]
Former [[Gosha]] |established = Unknown |main hall = Modified Kasuga-zukuri |alternative name = |札所等 = |festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |festival = Kami-gaeri festival (November 29) |map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}})
{{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭|12=draft}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|12=draft}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=draft}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|12=draft}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|12=draft}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=draft}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|12=draft}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|12=draft}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|12=draft}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|12=draft}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|12=draft}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=draft}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|12=draft}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|12=draft}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|12=draft}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|12=draft}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|12=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}}

Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft|12=draft}})

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft|12=draft}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|12=draft|12=draft}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|12=draft|12=draft}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|12=draft|12=draft}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft|12=draft}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|12=draft|12=draft}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|12=draft|12=draft}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|12=draft|12=draft}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft|12=draft}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|12=draft|12=draft}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft|12=draft}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|12=draft|12=draft}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft|12=draft}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|12=draft|12=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}}

The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|12=draft|12=draft}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft|12=draft}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|12=draft|12=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}}

Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft|12=draft}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|12=draft|12=draft}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|12=draft|12=draft}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|12=draft|12=draft}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|12=draft|12=draft}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft|12=draft}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|12=draft|12=draft}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft|12=draft}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|12=draft|12=draft}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|12=draft|12=draft}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines

Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|12=draft|12=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. {{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}}

revision.comment: 40 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:由良比女神社 up to rev 94621708
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 由良比女神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|由良比女神社 拝殿]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[島根県]][[隠岐郡]][[西ノ島町]]浦郷922 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[須勢理姫|須勢理姫命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（[[名神大社|名神大]]）
[[隠岐国]][[一宮]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 春日造変態 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月の最終土・日曜日 |神事 = 神帰祭 （[[11月29日]]） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=由良比女
神社|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}}}} '''由良比女神社'''（ゆらひめじんじゃ）は、[[島根県]][[隠岐郡]][[西ノ島町]]浦郷にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[名神大社]]）、[[隠岐国]][[一宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の1柱。 * [[須勢理姫|須勢理姫命]]、地元では由良比女大神。 『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では「由良比女神社 名神大 元名和多須神」として、元は「和多須神」である旨が記載されており、祭神は[[ワタツミ|海童神]]とも言われる。『[[袖中抄]]』や『[[土佐日記]]』には「ちぶり神」とある。 島前の伝承では、その昔、須勢理姫命が芋桶に乗って海を渡っているとき、海に浸した手を[[イカ]]が引っ張った（噛み付いたともいう）お詫びとして毎年社前の浜にイカが押し寄せるようになったと言う。 == 歴史 == === 概史 === 創建は不詳。 [[六国史|国史]]では、『[[続日本後紀]]』[[承和 (日本)|承和]]9年（[[842年]]）条において、由良比売命神（由良比女神社）・宇受加命神（[[中ノ島 (島根県)|中ノ島]]の[[宇受賀命神社]]）・水若酢命神（[[島後]]の[[水若酢神社]]）の3社が[[官社]]に預かる旨が記されている。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では隠岐国[[知夫郡]]に「由良比女神社 名神大 元名和多須神」と記載され、[[名神大社]]に列している。『隠岐国内神名帳』では「従三位上 由良姫大明神」と見える。 中世から江戸時代中期までは衰微。[[安永]]2年（[[1773年]]）、島前13村の庄屋により例大祭が復興された。近世以降は、隠岐国一宮を称した。 [[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に郷社に列した。社殿は小さかったが、明治22年（[[1889年]]）以降、社殿・境内地が整備された。 === 神階 === * 従三位上 （『隠岐国内神名帳』） - 表記は「由良姫大明神」。 == 祭祀 == === 神事 === * 由良比女神社例大祭 以前は二年に一度、7月の28日・29日に行われていた。現在は奇数年（元号）7月最終の土曜・日曜の夜に行なわれる西ノ島町（企画・編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム 運河のある町』[[山陰中央新報社]]、157頁西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』西ノ島町観光協会、15頁。二艘の大型漁船を連結させた神船に神輿を乗せ、船中で島前神楽を演じながら由良を出発し、浦郷湾へ向け海を渡る[[川本貢功]]『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[山陰中央新報社]]、139頁。神輿は町内を練り歩いた後、宮司宅で一泊し、翌日夜に再び海を渡り浦郷から神社に還御する。この往き帰りの神幸祭は島前島後の祭りのなかでは最も盛大な若者の神輿担ぎを見ることができる[[神村信幸]]『隠岐〜ふるさと案内記〜』[[臨川書店]]、141頁。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿（西ノ島町指定文化財）}}]] * 本殿 *: 明治22年（1889年）の造営。二間社[[春日造]]変態・[[向拝]][[唐破風]]。案内板によれば、以前は「[[大社造]]変態」と称したが、現在は「春日造変態」とされると言う。また[[出雲国|出雲]]では、この様式を「明神造」と呼ぶと言う。西ノ島町指定有形文化財に指定されている。 ファイル:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|境内社
{{small|拝殿西側。左が出雲大社、中央が恵比寿宮、右が龍蛇社。}} ファイル:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|一の鳥居
{{small|[[島根県道315号国賀海岸線|県道315号]]の脇。奥が[[随身|随身門]]。更に奥、随身門の陰に二の鳥居。}} ファイル:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|随身門
{{small|参道の左右に左大臣・右大臣像。}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|土俵
 == 文化財 == === 西ノ島町指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 本殿（建造物） - 1982年（昭和57年）3月7日指定[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html 西ノ島町指定文化財一覧] 島根県。 == 伝承 == [[ファイル:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|由良の浜（いか寄せの浜）}}]] 海上鳥居（右下）のある社前の由良の浜は、毎年10月から翌年2月にかけてイカが押し寄せ、「いか寄せの浜」とも呼ばれる。[[知夫村]]の伝承では、本来知夫村の烏賊浜にあった当社が由良に移ってからは、イカは由良に集まるようになったという。 祭神である由良比女様が苧桶に乗って、遠い海からやってきたところ手で海水をかき分けながら浦に向かってこられる途中、烏賊が由良比女様の手を引っ張ったりかみついたりした。そのお詫びとして、毎年秋になると烏賊の大群が神社の正面の浜辺に押し寄せるようになった[[野津龍]]『隠岐島の伝説』[[日本写真出版]]、215〜216頁西ノ島町小中学校教員会（編集）『ふるさと隠岐 西ノ島』西ノ島町小中学校教員会、87頁。地元の者は由良比女様の霊験と言って喜び、水際に番小屋や多くの小屋を建て、家族総出で烏賊を掬いとった西ノ島町立西ノ島中学校（編集）『郷土西ノ島』西ノ島町立西ノ島中学校、66頁。 元々、知夫里島の古海に鎮座していた由良比女神社が浦郷に移されてから、知夫里島に烏賊が寄らなくなったと伝えられている。また、西ノ島の者が知夫里島に烏賊が押し寄せるのをうらやましく思い、ご神体を盗んで浦郷地区に移転させたとも言われている。この神様は神武天皇の時代、烏賊を手に持ち苧桶に乗って神社の鳥居の近くの畳石に出現された、という伝説が残っている隠岐歴史民俗研究会『隠岐の国 散歩』隠岐観光協会、115〜116頁。 また一説に、この神様は須勢理姫命で、苧桶に乗って浦郷に来る途中で手を海水で洗った時、烏賊が戯れて神様の手に触ったため怒られたと言われている。毎年10月29日の由良比女様のお祭りの日は「神帰り」という御座入神事を行うが、烏賊が入り江に寄るのを「神帰り」の証としている。俗にこの神を「鯣大明神」というのはこのような烏賊との関係があったからである。 == 関連項目 == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|由良比女神社で行われる隠岐島前神楽]] * [[隠岐島前神楽]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆらひめしんしや}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:西ノ島町の建築物]] [[Category:西ノ島町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:隠岐国の式内社|名ゆらひめしんしや]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:島根県の旧郷社]] [[Category:隠岐国|社1]] [[Category:島根県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{{small|[[島根県道315号国賀海岸線|県道315号]]の脇。奥が[[随身|随身門]]。更に奥、随身門の陰に二の鳥居。}} ファイル:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|随身門
{{small|参道の左右に左大臣・右大臣像。}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|土俵
 == 文化財 == === 西ノ島町指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 本殿（建造物） - 1982年（昭和57年）3月7日指定[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html 西ノ島町指定文化財一覧] 島根県。 == 伝承 == [[ファイル:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|由良の浜（いか寄せの浜）}}]] 海上鳥居（右下）のある社前の由良の浜は、毎年10月から翌年2月にかけてイカが押し寄せ、「いか寄せの浜」とも呼ばれる。[[知夫村]]の伝承では、本来知夫村の烏賊浜にあった当社が由良に移ってからは、イカは由良に集まるようになったという。 祭神である由良比女様が苧桶に乗って、遠い海からやってきたところ手で海水をかき分けながら浦に向かってこられる途中、烏賊が由良比女様の手を引っ張ったりかみついたりした。そのお詫びとして、毎年秋になると烏賊の大群が神社の正面の浜辺に押し寄せるようになった[[野津龍]]『隠岐島の伝説』[[日本写真出版]]、215〜216頁西ノ島町小中学校教員会（編集）『ふるさと隠岐 西ノ島』西ノ島町小中学校教員会、87頁。地元の者は由良比女様の霊験と言って喜び、水際に番小屋や多くの小屋を建て、家族総出で烏賊を掬いとった西ノ島町立西ノ島中学校（編集）『郷土西ノ島』西ノ島町立西ノ島中学校、66頁。 元々、知夫里島の古海に鎮座していた由良比女神社が浦郷に移されてから、知夫里島に烏賊が寄らなくなったと伝えられている。また、西ノ島の者が知夫里島に烏賊が押し寄せるのをうらやましく思い、ご神体を盗んで浦郷地区に移転させたとも言われている。この神様は神武天皇の時代、烏賊を手に持ち苧桶に乗って神社の鳥居の近くの畳石に出現された、という伝説が残っている隠岐歴史民俗研究会『隠岐の国 散歩』隠岐観光協会、115〜116頁。 また一説に、この神様は須勢理姫命で、苧桶に乗って浦郷に来る途中で手を海水で洗った時、烏賊が戯れて神様の手に触ったため怒られたと言われている。毎年10月29日の由良比女様のお祭りの日は「神帰り」という御座入神事を行うが、烏賊が入り江に寄るのを「神帰り」の証としている。俗にこの神を「鯣大明神」というのはこのような烏賊との関係があったからである。 == 関連項目 == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|由良比女神社で行われる隠岐島前神楽]] * [[隠岐島前神楽]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆらひめしんしや}} [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:西ノ島町の建築物]] [[Category:西ノ島町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:隠岐国の式内社|名ゆらひめしんしや]] [[Category:一宮]] [[Category:島根県の旧郷社]] [[Category:隠岐国|社1]] [[Category:島根県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|qq=draft|qq=draft}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines

{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|由良比女神社|version=94621708|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|由良比女神社|version=94621708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (4).jpg |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]], [[Oki District]], [[Nishinoshima Town]], Urago 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Suseri-hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinai-sha]] ([[Myojin Taisha|Myojin Taisha]])
[[Ichinomiya]] of [[Oki Province]]
Former [[Gosha]] |established = Unknown |main hall = Modified Kasuga-zukuri |alternative name = |札所等 = |festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |festival = Kami-gaeri festival (November 29) |map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
{{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|qq=draft|qq=draft}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yurahime Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|由良比女神社|version=94621708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]

Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|qq=draft|qq=draft}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines

{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|由良比女神社|version=94621708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]

In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|qq=draft|qq=draft}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines

Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|由良比女神社|version=94621708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinai Shrines|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] }} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]
