It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|由良比女神社|version=94621708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinaisha|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] }} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (4).jpg |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]], [[Oki District]], [[Nishinoshima Town]], Urago 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Suseri-hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] ([[Myojin Taisha|Myojin Taisha]])
[[Ichinomiya]] of [[Oki Province]]
Former [[Gosha]] |established = Unknown |main hall = Modified Kasuga-zukuri |alternative name = |札所等 = |festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |festival = Kami-gaeri festival (November 29) |map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
{{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|qq=draft|qq=draft}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|由良比女神社|version=94621708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinaisha|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] }} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]

In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notsu Ryū|qq=}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki-Dozen Kagura|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|由良比女神社|version=94621708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinaisha|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] }} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]

Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]], [[Oki District]], [[Nishinoshima Town]], Urago 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[Suseri-hime|Suserihime no Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] ([[Myojin Taisha|Myojin Taisha]])
[[Ichinomiya]] of [[Oki Province]]
Former [[Gosha]] |established = Unknown |main hall = Modified Kasuga-zukuri |alternative name = |札所等 = |festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |festival = Kami-gaeri festival (November 29) |map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}})
{{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikarisai|qq=}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodenaka-shō|qq=}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tosa Nikki|qq=}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikkokushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kōki|qq=}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōwa (era)|qq=}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujika Shrine|qq=}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanoshima (Shimane)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuwakasu Shrine|qq=}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōgo Island|qq=}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki-Dozen Kagura|qq=}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamoto Mitsunori|qq=}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karahafu|qq=}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisha-zukuri|qq=}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notsu Ryū|qq=}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki-Dozen Kagura|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|由良比女神社|version=94621708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town Buildings]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinaisha|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] }} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]

In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki-Dozen Kagura|qq=}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamoto Mitsunori|qq=}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karahafu|qq=}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisha-zukuri|qq=}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notsu Ryū|qq=}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki-Dozen Kagura|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|由良比女神社|version=94621708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Nishinoshima Town History]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinaisha|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] }} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]

[[Ichinomiya]] of [[Oki Province]]
Former [[Gosha]] |established = Unknown |main hall = Modified Kasuga-zukuri |alternative name = |札所等 = |festival= Last Saturday and Sunday of July |festival = Kami-gaeri festival (November 29) |map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}})
{{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikarisai|qq=}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodenaka-shō|qq=}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tosa Nikki|qq=}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikkokushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kōki|qq=}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōwa (era)|qq=}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujika Shrine|qq=}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanoshima (Shimane)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuwakasu Shrine|qq=}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōgo Island|qq=}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki-Dozen Kagura|qq=}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamoto Mitsunori|qq=}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karahafu|qq=}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisha-zukuri|qq=}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notsu Ryū|qq=}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki-Dozen Kagura|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|由良比女神社|version=94621708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shikinaisha|Myojin Yurahime Shrine]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] }} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]

The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodenaka-shō|qq=}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tosa Nikki|qq=}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikkokushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kōki|qq=}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōwa (era)|qq=}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujika Shrine|qq=}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanoshima (Shimane)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuwakasu Shrine|qq=}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōgo Island|qq=}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki-Dozen Kagura|qq=}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamoto Mitsunori|qq=}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karahafu|qq=}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisha-zukuri|qq=}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notsu Ryū|qq=}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki-Dozen Kagura|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|由良比女神社|version=94621708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shimane Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Oki Province Shrines|1]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] }} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]

Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodenaka-shō|qq=}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tosa Nikki|qq=}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikkokushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kōki|qq=}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōwa (era)|qq=}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujika Shrine|qq=}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanoshima (Shimane)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuwakasu Shrine|qq=}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōgo Island|qq=}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki-Dozen Kagura|qq=}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamoto Mitsunori|qq=}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karahafu|qq=}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisha-zukuri|qq=}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines

Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodenaka-shō|qq=}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tosa Nikki|qq=}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikkokushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kōki|qq=}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōwa (era)|qq=}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujika Shrine|qq=}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanoshima (Shimane)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuwakasu Shrine|qq=}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōgo Island|qq=}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki-Dozen Kagura|qq=}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamoto Mitsunori|qq=}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karahafu|qq=}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisha-zukuri|qq=}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notsu Ryū|qq=}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki-Dozen Kagura|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|由良比女神社|version=94621708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yurahime Shrine]] }} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]

In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} |name = Yurahime Shrine |image = [[File:Yurahime jinja Haiden.JPEG|270px|Yurahime Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall |location = [[Shimane Prefecture]] [[Oki District]] [[Nishinoshima Town]], Uragō 922 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|5|30.9|N|132|59|16.2|E|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}})
{{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |Establishment = Unknown |Main Hall = Modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Temple Affiliation = |festival = Final Saturday and Sunday in July |Rituals = {{ill|Kamikarisai|ja|神帰祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikarisai|qq=}} (November 29) |Map = {{location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Yurahime
Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}), the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=}} (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodenaka-shō|qq=}} and {{ill|Tosa Nikki|ja|土佐日記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tosa Nikki|qq=}} refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikkokushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Zoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kōki|qq=}} mentions in {{ill|Jōwa (era)|ja|承和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōwa (era)|qq=}} 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujika Shrine|qq=}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanoshima (Shimane)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuwakasu Shrine|qq=}} on {{ill|Dōgo Island|ja|島後|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōgo Island|qq=}} were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=}} is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki-Dozen Kagura|qq=}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamoto Mitsunori|qq=}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}} architecture with a {{ill|Karahafu|ja|唐破風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karahafu|qq=}} gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of {{ill|Taisha-zukuri|ja|大社造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisha-zukuri|qq=}}, is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
{{small|From left: Izumo Taisha, Ebisu Shrine, and Ryūja Shrine.}} File:Yurahime jinja Gate.JPG|First Torii
{{small|Located by Prefectural Route 315.}} File:YurahimeJinja Zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate
{{small|Statues of left and right ministers flank the path.}} File:Oki Nishinoshima Yutahime Shrine ac (3).jpg|Sumo Ring == Cultural Properties == === Nishinoshima Town Designations === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ** Main Hall - Designated on March 7, 1982.[http://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/life/bunka/bunkazai/shimane/sityouson/nisinoshima.html List of Nishinoshima Town Cultural Properties], Shimane Prefecture == Folklore == [[File:Precincts of Yurahime jinja.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Yura Beach ("Squid Gathering Beach")}}]] The shrine's beach is famous for squid that gather annually from October to February, a phenomenon attributed to a legend involving the deity Suserihime and a squid.{{ill|Notsu Ryū|ja|野津龍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notsu Ryū|qq=}}『隠岐島の伝説』[[Nihon Shashin Shuppan]], pp. 215–216 == See Also == [[File:Oki-Dozen Kagura, Yurahime Shrine, Nishinoshima (6).jpg|thumb|220px|right|Oki-Dozen Kagura at Yurahime Shrine]] * {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki-Dozen Kagura|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yurahime-jinja}} * {{神道・神社史料集成|436001}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Oki Shrines}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{translated page|ja|由良比女神社|version=94621708|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Myojin Taisha]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Cultural Properties of Shimane Prefecture Municipalities]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:History of Nishinoshima Town]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Cultural properties of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:Yurahime Shrine]] [[sn:Yurahime Shrine]] [[en:Yurahime Shrine]] [[ja:由良比女神社]]

Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Yurahime Shrine''' (由良比女神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Uragō, [[Nishinoshima Town]], [[Oki District]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|qq=}}), the [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}, and was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deity is: * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}}, also locally known as Yurahime Ōkami. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly known as Watasu God." The deity is sometimes associated with [[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]] (sea deity). Records such as the {{ill|Sodenaka-shō|ja|袖中抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodenaka-shō|qq=}} and [[:en:Tosa Nikki|Tosa Nikki]] refer to it as the "Chiburi God." Local folklore recounts that {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suserihime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime|qq=}} once crossed the sea in a taro bucket and was pulled by a squid. In atonement, squid reportedly flock to the shrine’s shore every year. == History == === Early History === The shrine's founding date is unknown. In the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Rikkokushi]], [[:en:Shoku Nihon Kōki|Zoku Nihon Kōki]] mentions in [[:en:Jōwa|Jōwa (era)]] 9 (842 CE) that Yurahime Shrine, {{ill|Ujika Shrine|ja|宇受賀命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujika Shrine|qq=}} on {{ill|Nakanoshima (Shimane)|ja|中ノ島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanoshima (Shimane)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Mizuwakasu Shrine|Mizuwakasu Shrine]] on [[:en:Dōgojima|Dōgo Island]] were designated as official shrines. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in {{ill|Enchō (era)|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō (era)|qq=}} 5 (927 CE), lists the shrine as "Yurahime Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, formerly Watasu God." In the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, it is recorded as "Jusanmi no Kami Yurahime Daimyōjin." After a period of decline during the Middle Ages, the shrine's annual festival was revived in {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=}} 2 (1773) by the village leaders of the 13 local villages. In the modern era, it was designated as the [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Oki Province|ja|Oki Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Province|qq=}}. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} in 1872. The current shrine buildings were constructed and expanded beginning in 1889. === Divine Rank === * Jusanmi (Junior Third Rank) - Listed as "Yurahime Daimyōjin" in the {{ill|Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|ja|Oki Province 内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki Kokunai Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. == Rituals == === Festivals === * Yurahime Shrine festival: Formerly held biennially on July 28–29, it is now celebrated in odd-numbered years on the final Saturday and Sunday of July.西ノ島町（編集）『隠岐 西ノ島アルバム』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 157西ノ島町観光協会『隠岐・西ノ島町』Nishinoshima Town Tourism Association, p. 15 During the festival, a [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] is placed on a boat formed by linking two large fishing vessels. The mikoshi crosses the sea accompanied by the {{ill|Oki-Dozen Kagura|ja|隠岐島前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oki-Dozen Kagura|qq=}} and parades through the town before being brought back to the shrine.{{ill|Kawamoto Mitsunori|ja|川本貢功|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawamoto Mitsunori|qq=}}『隠岐 川本貢功写真集』[[San'in Chūō Shimbunsha]], p. 139 == Grounds == [[File:Yurahime jinja Honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Town-designated Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall: Built in 1889, featuring modified [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] architecture with a [[:en:Karahafu|Karahafu]] gable. The design, formerly described as a variation of [[:en:Taisha-zukuri|Taisha-zukuri]], is now referred to as "Kasuga-zukuri variation." The hall is a designated cultural property of Nishinoshima Town. File:Yurahime jinja keidaisha.JPG|Auxiliary Shrines
